results.title: Actuarial Analyst | Jobflarely
highlights:
# Actuarial Analyst
...
Verisk is a leading data analytics and technology partner to the global insurance industry, seeking an early-career analyst for their Strategic Actuarial Operations team. The role involves supporting internal and external responsibilities, applying actuarial techniques, and collaborating across business units to enhance the insurance industry's operations.
...
- Participate in initiatives to improve efficiency and automation of actuarial and business processes, learning best practices along the way
...
- Collaborate with other core lines business units and subject matter experts (actuarial, data and technology) to improve data quality, product development, actuarial procedures and U/X to help our customers overcome challenges
...
- Bachelor's degree with concentration in a quantitative discipline such as Statistics, Mathematics, Actuarial Science, Economics, Engineering, or Physical Sciences
...
- 1-3 years of P&C insurance industry experience, especially insurance data management, actuarial practices, and familiarity with the ISO/Verisk core business
...
- Strong analytical, problem solving, decision-making skills and technical aptitude
...
- Working knowledge of SQL, R, Python, or other general-purpose programming languages and familiarity with other BI tools (PowerBI, Tableau, ThoughtSpot)
...
- Verisk Analytics is a data analytics and risk assessment firm for the insurance sector. It was founded in 1971, and is headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey, USA, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is https://www.verisk.com.

And final rotation with Verisk 3E in Montreal with information extraction project ***** Rotation 1 : Verisk Health (now Verscend) ***** * Within the Population Health group at Verisk Health, played an integral role in establishing an accelerated feedback loop to identify model drift and the need for recalibration. Significantly reduced feedback time from one year to real-time by effectively detecting distribution shifts, ensuring timely adjustments and maintaining optimal performance. ***** Rotation 2 Verisk Claims ***** * Worked with Claims partners on their Auto MSA product. Fast-tracked their information extraction initiatives. * Utilized machine learning and predictive modeling (NLP) to build an intelligent CMS that expedites and streamlines the estimation process for claim values. * CMS collaborated with nurses, lawyers and doctors to enhance efficiency resulting in a reduction of median processing time from 335 to 240 minutes. ***** Rotation 3 Verisk 3E: sister company of Verisk in EHS space ***** * Developed and implemented advanced methods for extracting key information from Safety Data Sheets (SDS) and transforming it into a structured format, enabling efficient chemical compliance management and providing valuable data to customers. * Designed and implemented microservices architecture to enable serverless hosting of models, facilitating seamless autoscaling capabilities to efficiently process millions of documents * As a senior scientist, played a key role in the formation of the inaugural Data Science teams at the organization. Demonstrated leadership in cultivating a data-driven culture, educating stakeholders, and translating business problems to ML challenges

results.title: Lead Data Scientist – Property Catastrophe Modeling & Geo-Analytics | CareerNest
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist – Property Catastrophe Modeling & Geo-Analytics | CareerNest
...
# Lead Data Scientist – Property Catastrophe Modeling & Geo-Analytics
...
The Team Lead – Data Science is responsible for leading a team focused on analytical projects on identifying business opportunities, answering business questions and developing business solutions. The team member will need to be able to understand business needs and create research and implementation plans using data, statistical models and machine learning techniques.
...
### Responsibilities
...
• Direct analytical initiatives, leveraging data, statistical modeling, and machine learning to drive process improvements and decision-making.
...
• Provide analytical support and facilitate solution implementation through business or IT processes.
...
• Research and integrate new data sources and infrastructure, developing documentation and standards for easy business access.
...
• Create and optimize data repositories, reports, dashboards, and automated procedures to enhance efficiency and reduce costs.
...
• Leads a small team of data scientists (1–3), providing technical expertise and mentoring.
...
• Oversees development and application of algorithms/models to key business metrics, with the goal of improving operations or answering business questions.
...
• At least 5 years of experience in data mining and data/statistical analysis.
...
• At least 5 years experience with SQL and statistical software packages (Python, R, SAS) required.
...
• At least 5 years experience working as a data scientist or similar role, with proven track record in data processing, predictive modeling, and machine learning techniques
...
Knowledge and Skills:
...
• Demonstrated solid skills in data mining, machine learning, programming and optimization.
...
• Knowledge with cloud-based advanced data and analytics environment.
...
• Knowledge of working with AWS, GitHub or other cloud-based infrastructure.
...
• Solid experience using R, Python and other programing languages.
...
• Strong data skills and the ability to work with large structured and unstructured data sources.
...
• Solid experience in predictive modeling, general model building, and validation procedures.
...
• Solid experience in text mining or other text analytics techniques.

I’m a Senior Data Scientist with close to 6 years of experience building and deploying scalable machine learning products that drive measurable business outcomes. My work blends advanced analytics with strategic execution — from designing predictive models and optimizing large-scale data pipelines to leading cross-functional teams through end-to-end product delivery. At Verisk, I’ve worked extensively on data-driven solutions in the insurance space — including risk scoring, fraud detection, and pricing optimization — using technologies like Python, Spark, Databricks, AWS, and modern MLOps frameworks. These initiatives have improved model performance, enhanced risk segmentation, and streamlined decision-making for enterprise-scale systems. I’m passionate about applying data science to solve complex real-world problems, particularly those that involve high-impact risk assessment, operational efficiency, and automation. With a blend of technical depth and business acumen, I enjoy mentoring teams, exploring new data technologies, and translating insights into actionable strategies that create long-term value.

results.title: Verisk Data Engineer Interview Questions & Guide 2026
highlights:
Verisk Data Engineer Interview Questions & Guide 2026
...
# Verisk Data Engineer interview questions & guide 2026
...
### 1. What is a Data Engineer at Verisk?
...
As a **Data Engineer** at **Verisk**, you are the architect of the information infrastructure that powers the global insurance and financial services industries. Your work involves building, maintaining, and optimizing the complex data pipelines that transform massive, disparate datasets into actionable intelligence. At **Verisk**, data is not just an asset; it is the core product that enables risk assessment, predictive modeling, and strategic decision-making for clients worldwide.
...
You will operate at the intersection of high-scale data processing and business-critical analytics. Whether you are working with **Snowflake**, cloud-native environments like **AWS**, or complex **SQL**-based warehousing solutions, your role is to ensure data integrity, performance, and scalability. This position is vital for maintaining the competitive edge of **Verisk** products, requiring a mindset that balances rigorous engineering standards with an understanding of the downstream business impact of your data models.
...
### 3. Getting Ready for Your Interviews
...
Success at **Verisk** requires a blend
...
value of your engineering choices.
...
**Role-related Knowledge** – You must demonstrate a mastery of **SQL**, data warehousing principles, and cloud infrastructure. Interviewers look for candidates who can bridge the gap between low-level troubleshooting and high-level architectural design.
...
**Problem-
...
Ability** – You
...
**Communication and Justification** – **Verisk** values engineers who can explain their work
...
Do not just state what you did; explain why you chose a specific tool or methodology over alternatives, particularly regarding performance and maintenance.
...
This is the bedrock of the **Data Engineer** role at **Verisk**. You are expected to be fluent in advanced query optimization and schema design.
...
### 6. Key Responsibilities
...
As a **Data Engineer**, you will spend your time building and maintaining the "pipes" that move data across the enterprise. You will work closely with Data Scientists and Product Managers to ensure that the data they consume is accurate, timely, and well-structured. Typical projects include migrating on-premises databases to the cloud, optimizing existing ETL/ELT pipelines for speed, and developing automated monitoring tools to alert the team to data quality issues. You are expected to be proactive in identifying potential bottlenecks before they impact the business.
...
### 7. Role Requirements & Qualifications
...
* **Must-have skills**: Proficient in **SQL**, experience with at least one major cloud provider (**AWS** preferred), and a solid grasp of **ETL/ELT** development.
...
* **Nice-to-have skills**: Experience with orchestration tools (like Airflow), knowledge of Python for scripting, and exposure to big data frameworks.
...
* **Experience**: Typically, 3+ years of experience in a data-focused engineering role is preferred, with a track record of taking ownership of end-to-end data pipelines.
...
The **Data Engineer** position at **Verisk** is a high-impact role that serves as the backbone for the company’s analytical capabilities. By focusing your preparation on **SQL** mastery, **AWS** cloud proficiency, and the ability to clearly communicate your technical decision-making, you will be well-positioned to succeed in the interview process.
...

highlights:
Data Scientist, GenAI - Atlanta | Great Insurance Jobs
...
# Data Scientist, GenAI
...
At AIG, we are making long-term investments in a brand-new, innovative Generative AI team, designed to explore new possibilities for how artificial intelligence can be applied in insurance and beyond, and we need your help.
...
With the support and investment needed to explore new frontiers in generative AI, you'll be working alongside talented colleagues, innovating and leading projects that will transform how we manage risk and serve our customers.
...
team is central to our vision of the future and the core of our business offering
...
We will incorporate best-in-class engineering and product management
...
excited by the opportunity to create
...
we'd love to hear from
...
As a Data Scientist at AIG, you will serve a critical role in the architecture, development, and delivery of impactful enterprise-level Generative AI solutions. We are seeking a highly technical individual contributor with extensive hands-on experience, as we are an applied data science team operating in an agile and production-oriented environment. You will be focused on Large Language Models (LLMs) and Machine Learning (ML), acting as a primary technical driver for our most complex projects.
...
End-to-End Development: Lead the development and successful delivery of data science and generative AI solutions in accordance with business requirements.
...
Production Oversight: Monitor solutions in production to ensure performance, reliability, and accuracy.
...
Technical Collaboration: Partner with cross-functional teams including product managers, engineers, and business leaders to translate requirements into technical reality.
...
Evaluation & Quality: Build robust evaluation frameworks to measure LLM efficacy, manage ground truth dataset quality, and guide the product development roadmap.
...
Architecture: Design scalable ML pipelines and RAG frameworks that integrate seamlessly with enterprise data structures.
...
5+ years of experience in a data science role, with a strong emphasis on NLP and ML, working in an agile production-oriented environment.
...
Proven Delivery: Experience successfully delivering multiple GenAI, analytical, or ML projects from conception through to production.
...
Python Expertise: Strong, expert-level proficiency in Python and its data science ecosystem (e.g., PyTorch, Pandas, Scikit-learn).
...
LLM Mastery: 5+ years of practical experience with open-source Large Language Models (Llama 3, Mixtral, etc.), including prompt engineering, inference optimization, and fine-tuning.
...
GenAI Solutions: 3+ years of experience building Generative AI Solutions, including designing and building RAG frameworks, validation pipelines, observability, and monitoring solutions.
...
Education: Master's degree in data science, Computer Science, or a related quantitative field.
...
Palantir Platform Experience: Hands-on experience or certification in the Palantir platform (Foundry/AIP).
...
Ontology Mastery: A strong understanding of Ontology—specifically how to map complex real-world data entities and relationships into a digital twin framework to power AI applications.
...
A "hands-on" mindset with the desire to be a primary technical lead through code quality and architectural excellence.

# Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)
...
Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Experience: 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

We are seeking a highly accomplished Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics, along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

- Define and lead GenAI and data science strategy across business use cases.
- Design and implement LLM-based applications including RAG architectures.
- Lead development of machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions.

- Architect end-to-end ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring).
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.

- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.

results.title: Principal Data Scientist, Alpharetta, United States of America | Verizon Careers
highlights:
Principal Data Scientist, Alpharetta, United States of America | Verizon Careers
...
# Principal Data Scientist
...
- Alpharetta, Georgia
- Irving, Texas
- Basking Ridge, New Jersey
...
As a Principal Data Scientist in our Strategic Analytics & Modeling group, you are the intellectual engine translating complex data into executive-level strategy. Your mission is to deliver the "Deep Intelligence" that shapes our long-term business strategy. You will lead the development of advanced statistical models and rigorous analytical frameworks to solve unique business operations problems.
...
You aren’t just producing numbers; you are crafting the Business Case for our most significant investments. You will be a translator of complexity, leveraging LLMs and RAG architectures to synthesize vast amounts of internal data, turning the "noise" of global supply chain volatility, spend patterns, asset utilization etc. into clear, actionable insights for senior leadership. You will work in a high-maturity environment, leveraging Domino Data Lab and Gurobi to solve our most difficult optimization puzzles.
...
You will work with key stakeholders to generate hypotheses and create analytic models that answer our most impactful business questions. You are responsible for leading the design of data science products while thinking strategically about how these models meet the long-term goals of the business. You will leverage our full tech stack to develop resolutions to complex problems that require creativity, making decisions that directly impact Verizon's bottom line.
...
Architect AI-Driven Decision Systems: Design and implement RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems that ground LLMs in proprietary supply chain data to provide accurate, real-time strategic recommendations.
...
Optimize Complex Operations: Use Gurobi to solve high-dimensional supply chain constraints, moving beyond simple heuristics to find the mathematically "best" path for our global operations.
...
Accelerate with AI-Native Workflows: Utilize advanced coding assistants like Claude Code within VS Code to automate boilerplate and focus your energy on strategic problem-solving.
...
Drive Actionable Insights & Influencing: Lead end-to-end analytical projects, moving beyond "what happened" to prescribing solutions and proving the causal ROI of specific business interventions
...
Master Executive Storytelling: Use AI to help synthesize complex model outputs into high-impact narratives and "What-If" simulators that speak the language of the C-suite.
...
Partner Across the Ecosystem: Collaborate with our Innovation and Enablement teams to integrate models into a robust, strategically-aligned autonomous ecosystem.
...
You are a "Strategic Scientist" who sees AI as a force multiplier. You have the technical depth to build a complex model from scratch, but the business acumen to know when an explainable model is the more powerful choice for a stakeholder. You thrive on the challenge of influencing a "room full of skeptics" with data-backed conviction.
...
You’ll need to have:
...
Bachelor’s degree or four or more years of work experience.
...
Six or more years of relevant experience required, demonstrated through one or a combination of work and/or military experience, or specialized training.
...
Experience as a data scientist, analyst, or statistical modeler.
...
Experience using LLMs or coding agents (e.g., Claude Code, GitHub Copilot) to enhance productivity and code quality.
...
Experience with Google BigQuery, Teradata, Python/R, SQL, Alteryx, and visualization tools like Tableau.
...
Degree in business intelligence and analytics, computer science, or mathematics.
...
Master’s degree.
...
Proven track record of presenting complex technical findings to non-technical senior executives.
...
Experience with Data Warehousing or Teradata SQL.
...
Knowledge of distributive computing tools, like Hive or Spark.
...
Experience with programming languages, like Java or Scala.

results.title: Agentic AI Data Scientist | Hirevector
highlights: Data Scientist - GD08AE We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the future. Step into the future with The Hartford as an Agentic AI Data Scientist, where Generative AI isn't just a buzzword—it's central to our strategy. Join a team that's pioneering AI-driven solutions in underwriting, claims processing, and customer service, transforming how we operate and make decisions. As part of our Global Specialty Data Science & Analytics team, you'll tackle advanced use cases and shape the development of Gen AI capabilities across the organization. Here, your work will have a tangible impact, driving innovation and operational excellence with cutting-edge AI tools. If you're looking for a role where your expertise is not only valued but essential to our success, The Hartford is the place for you. We are looking for a technically proficient GenAI data scientist with deep implementation experience in building and evaluating GenAI systems, and a proven ability to stay at the forefront tools and techniques in the evolving AI landscape. This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work schedule. Candidates who live near one of our locations will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Responsibilities: Support The Hartford’s AI innovation strategy to identify and implement high-end use cases, ensuring alignment with business objectives and emerging industry trends. Support cutting-edge AI vision and roadmap for the enterprise to make The Hartford a leader in using AI for the insurance industry. Partner with stakeholders internal to the Data Science organization, Technology organization and business organization, aligning with business objectives and driving innovation to improve productivity and efficiency of underwriting and sales efforts. Design and build GenAI solutions for tasks such as summarization, question answering, search, data synthesis, etc. Apply AI techniques to low frequency, high severity exposures in Specialty Insurance. Rapidly prototype with cutting-edge tools, such as VertexAI/Google agent development kit, LangChain/LangGraph, RAG frameworks, HuggingFace, OpenAI APIs, etc. Research and recommend noval foundation model options, tools, and libraries to improve solutions in performance and reliability. Develop evaluation pipelines for generative outputs using a mix of automatic metrics, human feedback, and llm-as-a-judge methods. Collaborate with ML Ops to ensure best practice is followed for scalable training and deployment. Qualifications: Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Engineering or a related field. 3+ years of industry experience in machine learning or data science and with 1+ years focused on GenAI. Experience developing and deploying agentic AI workflows and systems. Proven experience in GenAI tools such as Vertex AI/Google agent development kit, LangChain/LangGraph, RAG frameworks, HuggingFace, OpenAI APIs, etc. Solid understanding of prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agent workflow, and LLM evaluation. Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc. Experience with vector databases such as Chroma, Pinecone, etc. and cloud platforms such as GCP, AWS, Azure, etc. Strong analytical, problem solving and debugging skills. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders across the enterprise. Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position. Compensation The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the
